content_markdown: Carbon dioxide is a greenhouse gas because

diagram_metadata.description: No diagram present in the image.

options.label: A
options.text: it absorbs infrared light radiated by the Sun.

options.label: B
options.text: it absorbs ultraviolet light radiated by the Earth.

options.label: C
options.text: its natural frequency molecular oscillation lies in the infrared region.

options.label: D
options.text: its natural frequency molecular oscillation lies in the ultraviolet region.

vibe_explanation: Greenhouse gases like $CO_2$ have molecular vibrational modes with natural frequencies that match the frequency of infrared radiation. This resonance allows them to absorb the long-wavelength (infrared) radiation emitted by the Earth's surface, rather than the short-wavelength radiation primarily emitted by the Sun.
